1	"""Session rename operations (User Story 5)."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from bids_utils._dataset import BIDSDataset
6	from bids_utils._scans import read_scans_tsv, write_scans_tsv
7	from bids_utils._types import Change, OperationResult
8	
9	
10	def rename_session(
11	    dataset: BIDSDataset,
12	    old: str,
13	    new: str,
14	    *,
15	    subject: str | None = None,
16	    dry_run: bool = False,
17	) -> OperationResult:
18	    """Rename a session. Use old="" for move-into-session.
19	
20	    Parameters
21	    ----------
22	    old, new
23	        Session labels WITHOUT "ses-" prefix. old="" means
24	        "introduce sessions where none exist".
25	    subject
26	        If specified, only rename for this subject. Otherwise all subjects.
27	    """
28	    result = OperationResult(dry_run=dry_run)
29	    old_id = f"ses-{old}" if old and not old.startswith("ses-") else old
30	    new_id = f"ses-{new}" if not new.startswith("ses-") else new
31	
32	    # Find subject directories to process
33	    if subject:
34	        sub_id = f"sub-{subject}" if not subject.startswith("sub-") else subject
35	        sub_dirs = [dataset.root / sub_id]
36	    else:
37	        sub_dirs = sorted(
38	            d
39	            for d in dataset.root.iterdir()
40	            if d.is_dir() and d.name.startswith("sub-")
41	        )
42	
43	    vcs = dataset.vcs
44	
45	    for sub_dir in sub_dirs:
46	        if not sub_dir.is_dir():
47	            continue
48	
49	        sub_name = sub_dir.name
50	
51	        if old_id:
52	            # Rename existing session
53	            old_ses_dir = sub_dir / old_id
54	            new_ses_dir = sub_dir / new_id
55	
56	            if not old_ses_dir.is_dir():
57	                result.warnings.append(
58	                    f"{sub_name}: session {old_id} not found, skipping"
59	                )
60	                continue
61	
62	            if new_ses_dir.exists():
63	                result.success = False
64	                result.errors.append(
65	                    f"{sub_name}: target session {new_id} already exists"
66	                )
67	                return result
68	
69	            result.changes.append(
70	                Change(
71	                    action="rename",
72	                    source=old_ses_dir,
73	                    target=new_ses_dir,
74	                    detail=f"{sub_name}: rename {old_id} → {new_id}",
75	                )
76	            )
77	
78	            if dry_run:
79	                continue
80	
81	            vcs.move(old_ses_dir, new_ses_dir)
82	
83	            # Rename files within the session
84	            old_label = old_id
85	            new_label = new_id
86	            for f in sorted(new_ses_dir.rglob("*"), reverse=True):
87	                if f.is_file() and old_label in f.name:
88	                    new_name = f.name.replace(old_label, new_label)
89	                    new_path = f.parent / new_name
90	                    if f != new_path:
91	                        vcs.move(f, new_path)
92	
93	            # Update scans.tsv
94	            for scans_file in new_ses_dir.rglob("*_scans.tsv"):
95	                rows = read_scans_tsv(scans_file)
96	                modified = False
97	                for row in rows:
98	                    fn = row.get("filename", "")
99	                    if old_label in fn:
100	                        row["filename"] = fn.replace(old_label, new_label)
101	                        modified = True
102	                if modified:
103	                    write_scans_tsv(scans_file, rows)
104	
105	        else:
106	            # Move into session: no existing session, introduce new one
107	            # Move datatype dirs into ses-X/
108	            new_ses_dir = sub_dir / new_id
109	            if new_ses_dir.exists():
110	                result.success = False
111	                result.errors.append(
112	                    f"{sub_name}: target session {new_id} already exists"
113	                )
114	                return result
115	
116	            # Find datatype directories (func/, anat/, fmap/, etc.)
117	            datatype_dirs = [
118	                d
119	                for d in sub_dir.iterdir()
120	                if d.is_dir() and not d.name.startswith("ses-")
121	            ]
122	
123	            if not datatype_dirs:
124	                result.warnings.append(f"{sub_name}: no datatype directories to move")
125	                continue
126	
127	            result.changes.append(
128	                Change(
129	                    action="create",
130	                    source=new_ses_dir,
131	                    detail=f"{sub_name}: create session directory {new_id}",
132	                )
133	            )
134	
135	            if dry_run:
136	                continue
137	
138	            new_ses_dir.mkdir()
139	
140	            # Move datatype dirs
141	            for dt_dir in datatype_dirs:
142	                target = new_ses_dir / dt_dir.name
143	                vcs.move(dt_dir, target)
144	
145	            # Rename files to include session entity
146	            new_ses_label = new_id
147	            for f in sorted(new_ses_dir.rglob("*"), reverse=True):
148	                if f.is_file() and sub_name in f.name and new_ses_label not in f.name:
149	                    # Insert ses-X after sub-XX
150	                    new_name = f.name.replace(
151	                        f"{sub_name}_", f"{sub_name}_{new_ses_label}_"
152	                    )
153	                    new_path = f.parent / new_name
154	                    if f != new_path:
155	                        vcs.move(f, new_path)
156	
157	            # Move scans.tsv if it exists at subject level
158	            sub_scans = sub_dir / f"{sub_name}_scans.tsv"
159	            if sub_scans.is_file():
160	                new_scans = new_ses_dir / f"{sub_name}_{new_ses_label}_scans.tsv"
161	                vcs.move(sub_scans, new_scans)
162	                # Update entries in scans.tsv
163	                rows = read_scans_tsv(new_scans)
164	                for row in rows:
165	                    fn = row.get("filename", "")
166	                    if sub_name in fn and new_ses_label not in fn:
167	                        # Update filenames in scans entries
168	                        parts = fn.split("/", 1)
169	                        if len(parts) == 2:
170	                            datatype, fname = parts
171	                            new_fname = fname.replace(
172	                                f"{sub_name}_", f"{sub_name}_{new_ses_label}_"
173	                            )
174	                            row["filename"] = f"{datatype}/{new_fname}"
175	                write_scans_tsv(new_scans, rows)
176	
177	    return result
178

1	"""File rename: core operation (User Story 1).
2	
3	Renames a BIDS file and all its sidecars, updates _scans.tsv,
4	and uses VCS when present.
5	"""
6	
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	from pathlib import Path
10	
11	from bids_utils._dataset import BIDSDataset
12	from bids_utils._scans import find_scans_tsv, update_scans_entry
13	from bids_utils._sidecars import find_sidecars
14	from bids_utils._types import BIDSPath, Change, OperationResult
15	
16	
17	def rename_file(
18	    dataset: BIDSDataset,
19	    path: str | Path,
20	    *,
21	    set_entities: dict[str, str] | None = None,
22	    drop_entities: list[str] | None = None,
23	    new_suffix: str | None = None,
24	    dry_run: bool = False,
25	    include_sourcedata: bool = False,
26	) -> OperationResult:
27	    """Rename a BIDS file and all its sidecars.
28	
29	    Parameters
30	    ----------
31	    dataset
32	        The BIDS dataset containing the file.
33	    path
34	        Path to the primary file (absolute or relative to dataset root).
35	    set_entities
36	        Entity key-value overrides (e.g., ``{"task": "nback"}``).
37	    drop_entities
38	        Entity keys to remove from the filename.
39	    new_suffix
40	        Optional new suffix (e.g., ``"T1w"``).
41	    dry_run
42	        If True, compute and return changes without modifying files.
43	    include_sourcedata
44	        If True, also rename matching files in sourcedata/.
45	
46	    Returns
47	    -------
48	    OperationResult
49	        Summary of changes made (or planned if dry_run).
50	    """
51	    result = OperationResult(dry_run=dry_run)
52	
53	    file_path = Path(path)
54	    if not file_path.is_absolute():
55	        file_path = dataset.root / file_path
56	
57	    if not file_path.exists():
58	        result.success = False
59	        result.errors.append(f"File not found: {file_path}")
60	        return result
61	
62	    # Parse the source filename
63	    bids_path = BIDSPath.from_path(file_path)
64	
65	    # Apply overrides
66	    if set_entities:
67	        bids_path = bids_path.with_entities(**set_entities)
68	    if drop_entities:
69	        remaining = {
70	            k: v for k, v in bids_path.entities.items() if k not in drop_entities
71	        }
72	        bids_path = BIDSPath(
73	            entities=remaining,
74	            suffix=bids_path.suffix,
75	            extension=bids_path.extension,
76	            datatype=bids_path.datatype,
77	        )
78	    if new_suffix:
79	        bids_path = bids_path.with_suffix(new_suffix)
80	
81	    new_filename = bids_path.to_filename()
82	    new_file_path = file_path.parent / new_filename
83	
84	    # Check no-op
85	    if file_path == new_file_path:
86	        result.warnings.append("Source and target are the same; nothing to do")
87	        return result
88	
89	    # Check for conflicts
90	    if new_file_path.exists():
91	        result.success = False
92	        result.errors.append(f"Target already exists: {new_file_path}")
93	        return result
94	
95	    # Collect all files to rename: primary + sidecars
96	    files_to_rename: list[tuple[Path, Path]] = [(file_path, new_file_path)]
97	
98	    sidecars = find_sidecars(file_path)
99	    for sidecar in sidecars:
100	        old_stem, _ = _split_stem_ext(sidecar.name)
101	        new_stem, _ = _split_stem_ext(new_filename)
102	        # Sidecar keeps its own extension but gets the new stem
103	        new_sidecar_name = new_stem + _get_extension(sidecar.name)
104	        new_sidecar_path = sidecar.parent / new_sidecar_name
105	
106	        if new_sidecar_path.exists() and new_sidecar_path != sidecar:
107	            result.success = False
108	            result.errors.append(f"Sidecar target already exists: {new_sidecar_path}")
109	            return result
110	
111	        files_to_rename.append((sidecar, new_sidecar_path))
112	
113	    # Record changes
114	    for old, new in files_to_rename:
115	        result.changes.append(
116	            Change(
117	                action="rename",
118	                source=old,
119	                target=new,
120	                detail=f"Rename {old.name} → {new.name}",
121	            )
122	        )
123	
124	    # Update _scans.tsv
125	    scans_path = find_scans_tsv(file_path, dataset.root)
126	    if scans_path is not None:
127	        # Compute the relative path as stored in _scans.tsv
128	        scans_dir = scans_path.parent
129	        try:
130	            old_rel = str(file_path.relative_to(scans_dir))
131	            new_rel = str(new_file_path.relative_to(scans_dir))
132	        except ValueError:
133	            old_rel = ""
134	            new_rel = ""
135	
136	        if old_rel and new_rel:
137	            result.changes.append(
138	                Change(
139	                    action="modify",
140	                    source=scans_path,
141	                    detail=f"Update _scans.tsv: {old_rel} → {new_rel}",
142	                )
143	            )
144	
145	    if dry_run:
146	        return result
147	
148	    # Execute renames
149	    vcs = dataset.vcs
150	    for old, new in files_to_rename:
151	        vcs.move(old, new)
152	
153	    # Update _scans.tsv
154	    if scans_path is not None and old_rel and new_rel:
155	        update_scans_entry(scans_path, old_rel, new_rel)
156	
157	    return result
158	
159	
160	def _split_stem_ext(filename: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
161	    """Split filename into stem and extension, handling .nii.gz."""
162	    for compound in (".nii.gz", ".tsv.gz"):
163	        if filename.endswith(compound):
164	            return filename[: -len(compound)], compound
165	    parts = filename.rsplit(".", 1)
166	    if len(parts) == 2:
167	        return parts[0], "." + parts[1]
168	    return filename, ""
169	
170	
171	def _get_extension(filename: str) -> str:
172	    """Get the extension from a filename, handling .nii.gz."""
173	    _, ext = _split_stem_ext(filename)
174	    return ext
175

"""File rename: core operation (User Story 1).

Renames a BIDS file and all its sidecars, updates _scans.tsv,
and uses VCS when present.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

from bids_utils._dataset import BIDSDataset
from bids_utils._scans import find_scans_tsv, update_scans_entry
from bids_utils._sidecars import find_sidecars
from bids_utils._types import BIDSPath, Change, OperationResult


def rename_file(
    dataset: BIDSDataset,
    path: str | Path,
    *,
    set_entities: dict[str, str] | None = None,
    drop_entities: list[str] | None = None,
    new_suffix: str | None = None,
    dry_run: bool = False,
    include_sourcedata: bool = False,
) -> OperationResult:
    """Rename a BIDS file and all its sidecars.

    Parameters
    ----------
    dataset
        The BIDS dataset containing the file.
    path
        Path to the primary file (absolute or relative to dataset root).
    set_entities
        Entity key-value overrides (e.g., ``{"task": "nback"}``).
    drop_entities
        Entity keys to remove from the filename.
    new_suffix
        Optional new suffix (e.g., ``"T1w"``).
    dry_run
        If True, compute and return changes without modifying files.
    include_sourcedata
        If True, also rename matching files in sourcedata/.

    Returns
    -------
    OperationResult
        Summary of changes made (or planned if dry_run).
    """
    result = OperationResult(dry_run=dry_run)

    file_path = Path(path)
    if not file_path.is_absolute():
        file_path = dataset.root / file_path

    if not file_path.exists():
        result.success = False
        result.errors.append(f"File not found: {file_path}")
        return result

    # Parse the source filename
    bids_path = BIDSPath.from_path(file_path)

    # Apply overrides
    if set_entities:
        bids_path = bids_path.with_entities(**set_entities)
    if drop_entities:
        remaining = {
            k: v for k, v in bids_path.entities.items() if k not in drop_entities
        }
        bids_path = BIDSPath(
            entities=remaining,
            suffix=bids_path.suffix,
            extension=bids_path.extension,
            datatype=bids_path.datatype,
        )
    if new_suffix:
        bids_path = bids_path.with_suffix(new_suffix)

    new_filename = bids_path.to_filename()
    new_file_path = file_path.parent / new_filename

    # Check no-op
    if file_path == new_file_path:
        result.warnings.append("Source and target are the same; nothing to do")
        return result

    # Check for conflicts
    if new_file_path.exists():
        result.success = False
        result.errors.append(f"Target already exists: {new_file_path}")
        return result

    # Collect all files to rename: primary + sidecars
    files_to_rename: list[tuple[Path, Path]] = [(file_path, new_file_path)]

    sidecars = find_sidecars(file_path)
    for sidecar in sidecars:
        old_stem, _ = _split_stem_ext(sidecar.name)
        new_stem, _ = _split_stem_ext(new_filename)
        # Sidecar keeps its own extension but gets the new stem
        new_sidecar_name = new_stem + _get_extension(sidecar.name)
        new_sidecar_path = sidecar.parent / new_sidecar_name

        if new_sidecar_path.exists() and new_sidecar_path != sidecar:
            result.success = False
            result.errors.append(f"Sidecar target already exists: {new_sidecar_path}")
            return result

        files_to_rename.append((sidecar, new_sidecar_path))

    # Record changes
    for old, new in files_to_rename:
        result.changes.append(
            Change(
                action="rename",
                source=old,
                target=new,
                detail=f"Rename {old.name} → {new.name}",
            )
        )

    # Update _scans.tsv
    scans_path = find_scans_tsv(file_path, dataset.root)
    if scans_path is not None:
        # Compute the relative path as stored in _scans.tsv
        scans_dir = scans_path.parent
        try:
            old_rel = str(file_path.relative_to(scans_dir))
            new_rel = str(new_file_path.relative_to(scans_dir))
        except ValueError:
            old_rel = ""
            new_rel = ""

        if old_rel and new_rel:
            result.changes.append(
                Change(
                    action="modify",
                    source=scans_path,
                    detail=f"Update _scans.tsv: {old_rel} → {new_rel}",
                )
            )

    if dry_run:
        return result

    # Execute renames
    vcs = dataset.vcs
    for old, new in files_to_rename:
        vcs.move(old, new)

    # Update _scans.tsv
    if scans_path is not None and old_rel and new_rel:
        update_scans_entry(scans_path, old_rel, new_rel)

    return result


def _split_stem_ext(filename: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Split filename into stem and extension, handling .nii.gz."""
    for compound in (".nii.gz", ".tsv.gz"):
        if filename.endswith(compound):
            return filename[: -len(compound)], compound
    parts = filename.rsplit(".", 1)
    if len(parts) == 2:
        return parts[0], "." + parts[1]
    return filename, ""


def _get_extension(filename: str) -> str:
    """Get the extension from a filename, handling .nii.gz."""
    _, ext = _split_stem_ext(filename)
    return ext

"""Subject rename and remove operations (User Stories 4, 7)."""

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

from bids_utils._dataset import BIDSDataset
from bids_utils._participants import remove_participant, rename_participant
from bids_utils._scans import read_scans_tsv, write_scans_tsv
from bids_utils._types import (
    Change,
    OperationResult,
    normalize_subject_id,
    rename_change,
    require_subject_dir,
)


def rename_subject(
    dataset: BIDSDataset,
    old: str,
    new: str,
    *,
    dry_run: bool = False,
    include_sourcedata: bool = False,
) -> OperationResult:
    """Rename a subject across the entire dataset.

    Parameters
    ----------
    old, new
        Subject labels WITHOUT "sub-" prefix (e.g., "01", "99").
    """
    result = OperationResult(dry_run=dry_run)
    old_id = normalize_subject_id(old)
    new_id = normalize_subject_id(new)

    old_dir = require_subject_dir(dataset.root, old_id, result)
    if old_dir is None:
        return result
    new_dir = dataset.root / new_id

    if new_dir.exists():
        result.success = False
        result.errors.append(f"Target subject already exists: {new_dir}")
        return result

    # Collect all files that need renaming
    files_to_rename: list[Path] = []
    for f in sorted(old_dir.rglob("*")):
        if f.is_file() and old_id in f.name:
            files_to_rename.append(f)

    # Record directory rename
    result.changes.append(
        rename_change(old_dir, new_dir, f"Rename directory {old_id} \u2192 {new_id}")
    )

    # Record file renames
    for f in files_to_rename:
        new_name = f.name.replace(old_id, new_id)
        # Compute target path (under new_dir)
        rel = f.relative_to(old_dir)
        new_path = new_dir / rel.parent / new_name
        result.changes.append(
            rename_change(f, new_path, f"Rename {f.name} \u2192 {new_name}")
        )

    # participants.tsv update
    participants = dataset.root / "participants.tsv"
    if participants.is_file():
        result.changes.append(
            Change(
                action="modify",
                source=participants,
                detail=f"Update participants.tsv: {old_id} → {new_id}",
            )
        )

    # scans.tsv updates
    for scans_file in old_dir.rglob("*_scans.tsv"):
        new_scans_name = scans_file.name.replace(old_id, new_id)
        result.changes.append(
            Change(
                action="modify",
                source=scans_file,
                detail=f"Update scans.tsv entries and rename to {new_scans_name}",
            )
        )

    if dry_run:
        return result

    # Execute: rename the directory first
    vcs = dataset.vcs
    vcs.move(old_dir, new_dir)

    # Rename files within the new directory
    for f in sorted(new_dir.rglob("*"), reverse=True):
        if f.is_file() and old_id in f.name:
            new_name = f.name.replace(old_id, new_id)
            new_path = f.parent / new_name
            if f != new_path:
                vcs.move(f, new_path)

    # Update scans.tsv files (they're now under new_dir)
    for scans_file in sorted(new_dir.rglob("*_scans.tsv")):
        rows = read_scans_tsv(scans_file)
        modified = False
        for row in rows:
            fn = row.get("filename", "")
            if old_id in fn:
                row["filename"] = fn.replace(old_id, new_id)
                modified = True
        if modified:
            write_scans_tsv(scans_file, rows)

    # Update participants.tsv
    if participants.is_file():
        rename_participant(participants, old_id, new_id)

    # Handle sourcedata if requested
    if include_sourcedata:
        for extra_dir_name in ["sourcedata", ".heudiconv"]:
            extra = dataset.root / extra_dir_name / old_id
            new_extra = dataset.root / extra_dir_name / new_id
            if extra.is_dir() and not new_extra.exists():
                vcs.move(extra, new_extra)

    return result


def remove_subject(
    dataset: BIDSDataset,
    subject: str,
    *,
    dry_run: bool = False,
    force: bool = False,
) -> OperationResult:
    """Remove a subject from the dataset."""
    result = OperationResult(dry_run=dry_run)
    sub_id = normalize_subject_id(subject)

    sub_dir = require_subject_dir(dataset.root, sub_id, result)
    if sub_dir is None:
        return result

    result.changes.append(
        Change(action="delete", source=sub_dir, detail=f"Remove {sub_id} directory")
    )

    participants = dataset.root / "participants.tsv"
    if participants.is_file():
        result.changes.append(
            Change(
                action="modify",
                source=participants,
                detail=f"Remove {sub_id} from participants.tsv",
            )
        )

    if dry_run:
        return result

    vcs = dataset.vcs
    vcs.remove(sub_dir)

    if participants.is_file():
        remove_participant(participants, sub_id)

    return result

1	"""Metadata aggregate/segregate/audit operations (User Story 6).
2	
3	Uses BIDS inheritance hierarchy to manage metadata distribution.
4	"""
5	
6	from __future__ import annotations
7	
8	import contextlib
9	import json
10	from collections import defaultdict
11	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Literal
14	
15	from bids_utils._dataset import BIDSDataset
16	from bids_utils._types import Change, OperationResult
17	
18	
19	@dataclass
20	class AuditResult:
21	    """Result of a metadata audit."""
22	
23	    inconsistent_keys: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = field(default_factory=dict)
24	    total_files: int = 0
25	
26	
27	def _find_json_sidecars(root: Path, scope: Path | None = None) -> list[Path]:
28	    """Find all JSON sidecar files (not dataset_description.json)."""
29	    search = scope or root
30	    return sorted(
31	        f
32	        for f in search.rglob("*.json")
33	        if f.name != "dataset_description.json"
34	        and not any(p.startswith(".") for p in f.relative_to(root).parts)
35	    )
36	
37	
38	def _group_by_stem_suffix(files: list[Path]) -> dict[str, list[Path]]:
39	    """Group JSON files by their suffix (e.g., _bold.json, _T1w.json)."""
40	    groups: dict[str, list[Path]] = defaultdict(list)
41	    for f in files:
42	        # Extract suffix: last underscore-separated part before .json
43	        stem = f.stem  # e.g., sub-01_task-rest_bold
44	        parts = stem.rsplit("_", 1)
45	        suffix = parts[-1] if len(parts) > 1 else stem
46	        groups[suffix].append(f)
47	    return dict(groups)
48	
49	
50	def _find_common_keys(json_files: list[Path]) -> dict[str, Any]:
51	    """Find key-value pairs common to ALL files."""
52	    if not json_files:
53	        return {}
54	
55	    # Load all files
56	    all_data: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
57	    for f in json_files:
58	        try:
59	            data = json.loads(f.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
60	            if isinstance(data, dict):
61	                all_data.append(data)
62	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
63	            return {}  # Can't determine common keys if a file is unreadable
64	
65	    if len(all_data) != len(json_files):
66	        return {}  # Some files missing or unreadable
67	
68	    if not all_data:
69	        return {}
70	
71	    # Keys present in ALL files with identical values
72	    common: dict[str, Any] = {}
73	    candidate_keys = set(all_data[0].keys())
74	    for data in all_data[1:]:
75	        candidate_keys &= set(data.keys())
76	
77	    for key in candidate_keys:
78	        values = [data[key] for data in all_data]
79	        if all(v == values[0] for v in values):
80	            common[key] = values[0]
81	
82	    return common
83	
84	
85	def aggregate_metadata(
86	    dataset: BIDSDataset,
87	    *,
88	    scope: str | Path | None = None,
89	    mode: Literal["copy", "move"] = "move",
90	    dry_run: bool = False,
91	) -> OperationResult:
92	    """Hoist common metadata up the inheritance hierarchy.
93	
94	    Parameters
95	    ----------
96	    scope
97	        Restrict to a subdirectory (e.g., "sub-01/").
98	    mode
99	        "move" removes keys from leaf files; "copy" keeps them.
100	    """
101	    result = OperationResult(dry_run=dry_run)
102	
103	    scope_path = Path(scope) if scope else None
104	    if scope_path and not scope_path.is_absolute():
105	        scope_path = dataset.root / scope_path
106	
107	    json_files = _find_json_sidecars(dataset.root, scope_path)
108	    groups = _group_by_stem_suffix(json_files)
109	
110	    for suffix, files in groups.items():
111	        if len(files) < 2:
112	            continue
113	
114	        common = _find_common_keys(files)
115	        if not common:
116	            continue
117	
118	        # Determine the parent directory for the aggregated sidecar
119	        # Use the longest common parent directory
120	        parents = [f.parent for f in files]
121	        common_parent = parents[0]
122	        for p in parents[1:]:
123	            while not str(p).startswith(str(common_parent)):
124	                common_parent = common_parent.parent
125	                if common_parent == dataset.root.parent:
126	                    break
127	
128	        # Target: parent_dir/suffix.json (e.g., bold.json)
129	        target = common_parent / f"{suffix}.json"
130	
131	        result.changes.append(
132	            Change(
133	                action="create" if not target.exists() else "modify",
134	                source=target,
135	                detail=(
136	                    f"Aggregate {len(common)} key(s) to "
137	                    f"{target.relative_to(dataset.root)}: {list(common.keys())}"
138	                ),
139	            )
140	        )
141	
142	        if dry_run:
143	            continue
144	
145	        # Write/update the parent-level sidecar
146	        existing: dict[str, Any] = {}
147	        if target.exists():
148	            with contextlib.suppress(json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
149	                existing = json.loads(target.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
150	        existing.update(common)
151	        target.write_text(json.dumps(existing, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
152	
153	        # Remove keys from leaf files (if mode="move")
154	        if mode == "move":
155	            for f in files:
156	                try:
157	                    data = json.loads(f.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
158	                except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
159	                    continue
160	                modified = False
161	                for key in common:
162	                    if key in data:
163	                        del data[key]
164	                        modified = True
165	                if modified:
166	                    f.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
167	
168	    return result
169	
170	
171	def segregate_metadata(
172	    dataset: BIDSDataset,
173	    *,
174	    scope: str | Path | None = None,
175	    dry_run: bool = False,
176	) -> OperationResult:
177	    """Push all metadata down to leaf-level sidecars.
178	
179	    This is the inverse of aggregate: for each data file, resolve
180	    the full inheritance chain and write a self-contained sidecar.
181	    """
182	    result = OperationResult(dry_run=dry_run)
183	
184	    scope_path = Path(scope) if scope else None
185	    if scope_path and not scope_path.is_absolute():
186	        scope_path = dataset.root / scope_path
187	
188	    search = scope_path or dataset.root
189	
190	    # Find all data files (non-JSON, non-TSV)
191	    data_files = sorted(
192	        f
193	        for f in search.rglob("*")
194	        if f.is_file()
195	        and f.suffix in (".gz", "")
196	        and not f.name.endswith(".json")
197	        and not f.name.endswith(".tsv")
198	        and "sub-" in f.name
199	    )
200	
201	    for data_file in data_files:
202	        # Find the JSON sidecar for this data file
203	        stem = data_file.name
204	        for ext in (".nii.gz", ".nii"):
205	            if stem.endswith(ext):
206	                stem = stem[: -len(ext)]
207	                break
208	
209	        leaf_json = data_file.parent / f"{stem}.json"
210	
211	        # Resolve metadata through inheritance chain
212	        resolved = _resolve_inheritance(data_file, dataset.root)
213	
214	        if not resolved:
215	            continue
216	
217	        result.changes.append(
218	            Change(
219	                action="modify" if leaf_json.exists() else "create",
220	                source=leaf_json,
221	                detail=f"Segregate metadata to {leaf_json.name}",
222	            )
223	        )
224	
225	        if dry_run:
226	            continue
227	
228	        leaf_json.write_text(json.dumps(resolved, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
229	
230	    return result
231	
232	
233	def _resolve_inheritance(data_file: Path, dataset_root: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
234	    """Resolve metadata through the BIDS inheritance chain."""
235	    # Extract suffix from filename
236	    stem = data_file.name
237	    for ext in (".nii.gz", ".nii", ".tsv.gz"):
238	        if stem.endswith(ext):
239	            stem = stem[: -len(ext)]
240	            break
241	    else:
242	        stem = data_file.stem
243	
244	    parts = stem.rsplit("_", 1)
245	    suffix = parts[-1] if len(parts) > 1 else stem
246	
247	    # Walk from dataset root down to the file's directory
248	    resolved: dict[str, Any] = {}
249	    current = dataset_root
250	    file_dir = data_file.parent
251	
252	    # Collect directories from root to file
253	    dirs = [dataset_root]
254	    rel = file_dir.relative_to(dataset_root)
255	    for part in rel.parts:
256	        current = current / part
257	        dirs.append(current)
258	
259	    for d in dirs:
260	        # Check for suffix.json at each level
261	        sidecar = d / f"{suffix}.json"
262	        if sidecar.is_file():
263	            try:
264	                data = json.loads(sidecar.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
265	                if isinstance(data, dict):
266	                    resolved.update(data)
267	            except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
268	                pass
269	
270	    # Finally, the leaf-level sidecar (file-specific)
271	    leaf = data_file.parent / f"{stem}.json"
272	    if leaf.is_file():
273	        try:
274	            data = json.loads(leaf.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
275	            if isinstance(data, dict):
276	                resolved.update(data)
277	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
278	            pass
279	
280	    return resolved
281	
282	
283	def audit_metadata(dataset: BIDSDataset) -> AuditResult:
284	    """Report metadata keys that are neither fully unique nor fully equivalent.
285	
286	    These indicate potential acquisition inconsistencies.
287	    """
288	    result = AuditResult()
289	
290	    json_files = _find_json_sidecars(dataset.root)
291	    result.total_files = len(json_files)
292	
293	    groups = _group_by_stem_suffix(json_files)
294	
295	    for suffix, files in groups.items():
296	        if len(files) < 2:
297	            continue
298	
299	        # Collect all key-value pairs
300	        all_data: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
301	        for f in files:
302	            try:
303	                data = json.loads(f.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
304	                if isinstance(data, dict):
305	                    all_data.append(data)
306	            except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
307	                continue
308	
309	        if len(all_data) < 2:
310	            continue
311	
312	        # Check each key
313	        all_keys: set[str] = set()
314	        for data in all_data:
315	            all_keys.update(data.keys())
316	
317	        for key in all_keys:
318	            values = [data.get(key) for data in all_data if key in data]
319	            if not values:
320	                continue
321	
322	            # Skip if all same (fully equivalent) or all different (fully unique)
323	            unique_values = {json.dumps(v, sort_keys=True) for v in values}
324	            if len(unique_values) == 1 or len(unique_values) == len(values):
325	                continue
326	
327	            # This key has inconsistent values
328	            result.inconsistent_keys[f"{suffix}/{key}"] = [
329	                {"file": str(f), "value": data.get(key)}
330	                for f, data in zip(files, all_data, strict=False)
331	                if key in data
332	            ]
333	
334	    return result
335

1	"""Schema-driven migration for BIDS datasets (User Stories 2, 3).
2	
3	Handles 1.x deprecation fixes and 2.0 migration using rules derived
4	from bidsschematools.
5	"""
6	
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	import json
10	import re
11	from collections.abc import Callable
12	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
13	from pathlib import Path
14	from typing import Any
15	
16	from bids_utils._dataset import BIDSDataset
17	from bids_utils._scans import find_scans_tsv, read_scans_tsv, write_scans_tsv
18	from bids_utils._types import BIDSPath, Change
19	
20	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	# Data model
22	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
23	
24	
25	@dataclass
26	class MigrationRule:
27	    """A single migration rule."""
28	
29	    id: str
30	    from_version: str
31	    category: str  # field_rename, value_rename, suffix_rename, etc.
32	    description: str
33	    old_field: str | None = None
34	    new_field: str | None = None
35	    old_value: str | None = None
36	    new_value: str | None = None
37	    affected_suffixes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
38	    metadata_key: str | None = None  # for value renames: which metadata key
39	    handler: Callable[..., list[MigrationFinding]] | None = field(
40	        default=None, repr=False
41	    )
42	
43	
44	@dataclass
45	class MigrationFinding:
46	    """A specific instance where a rule matches a file."""
47	
48	    rule: MigrationRule
49	    file: Path
50	    current_value: Any
51	    proposed_value: Any
52	    can_auto_fix: bool = True
53	    reason: str | None = None
54	
55	
56	@dataclass
57	class MigrationResult:
58	    """Result of a migrate operation."""
59	
60	    success: bool = True
61	    dry_run: bool = False
62	    from_version: str = ""
63	    to_version: str = ""
64	    findings: list[MigrationFinding] = field(default_factory=list)
65	    changes: list[Change] = field(default_factory=list)
66	    warnings: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
67	    errors: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
68	
69	
70	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
71	# Migration registry
72	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
73	
74	_RULES: list[MigrationRule] = []
75	
76	
77	def _register_rule(rule: MigrationRule) -> None:
78	    _RULES.append(rule)
79	
80	
81	def _get_rules(
82	    from_version: str, to_version: str, *, major_only: bool = False
83	) -> list[MigrationRule]:
84	    """Get applicable rules between two versions.
85	
86	    Parameters
87	    ----------
88	    from_version
89	        Current dataset version.
90	    to_version
91	        Target version.
92	    major_only
93	        If True, only return rules for the target major version
94	        (e.g., only 2.0 rules, not 1.x rules).
95	    """
96	    from packaging.version import InvalidVersion, Version
97	
98	    try:
99	        from_v = Version(from_version)
100	        to_v = Version(to_version)
101	    except InvalidVersion:
102	        return []
103	
104	    applicable = []
105	    for rule in _RULES:
106	        try:
107	            rule_v = Version(rule.from_version)
108	        except Exception:
109	            continue
110	
111	        if major_only:
112	            # Only include rules whose major version matches the target
113	            if rule_v.major != to_v.major:
114	                continue
115	            if rule_v <= to_v:
116	                applicable.append(rule)
117	        else:
118	            if from_v < rule_v <= to_v or rule_v <= from_v <= to_v:
119	                applicable.append(rule)
120	
121	    return applicable
122	
123	
124	def _is_major_version_upgrade(from_version: str, to_version: str) -> bool:
125	    """Check if migration crosses a major version boundary."""
126	    from packaging.version import InvalidVersion, Version
127	
128	    try:
129	        from_v = Version(from_version)
130	        to_v = Version(to_version)
131	    except InvalidVersion:
132	        return False
133	    return to_v.major > from_v.major
134	
135	
136	def _latest_1x_version() -> str:
137	    """Return the latest known 1.x BIDS version."""
138	    return "1.11.1"

311	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
312	# BIDS 2.0 migration rules (placeholder infrastructure)
313	#
314	# The BIDS 2.0 schema is not yet finalized.  The rules below register the
315	# *categories* of change that 2.0 will require so that the engine, scanner,
316	# applier, and test infrastructure are exercised end-to-end.  Concrete rules
317	# will be added once the 2.0 schema stabilizes.
318	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
319	
320	# NOTE: No concrete 2.0 rules are registered yet because the schema is not
321	# finalized.  When rules are added they should use from_version="2.0.0" and
322	# one of the 2.0-specific categories below:
323	#   - "entity_rename"         (entity key changes, e.g. hypothetical acq→acquisition)
324	#   - "structural_reorg"      (directory layout changes)
325	#   - "metadata_key_change"   (metadata key renames specific to 2.0)

328	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
329	# Scanning and fixing logic
330	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
331	
332	
333	def _scan_json_files(dataset_root: Path) -> list[Path]:
334	    """Find all JSON sidecar files in the dataset."""
335	    return sorted(dataset_root.rglob("*.json"))
336	
337	
338	def _scan_for_field_rename(
339	    json_files: list[Path],
340	    rule: MigrationRule,
341	) -> list[MigrationFinding]:
342	    """Scan for deprecated metadata field names."""
343	    findings: list[MigrationFinding] = []
344	    for jf in json_files:
345	        try:
346	            data = json.loads(jf.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
347	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
348	            continue
349	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
350	            continue
351	        if rule.old_field and rule.old_field in data:
352	            findings.append(
353	                MigrationFinding(
354	                    rule=rule,
355	                    file=jf,
356	                    current_value=f"{rule.old_field}: {data[rule.old_field]}",
357	                    proposed_value=f"{rule.new_field}: {data[rule.old_field]}",
358	                )
359	            )
360	    return findings
361	
362	
363	def _scan_for_enum_rename(
364	    json_files: list[Path],
365	    rule: MigrationRule,
366	) -> list[MigrationFinding]:
367	    """Scan for deprecated enum values."""
368	    findings: list[MigrationFinding] = []
369	    for jf in json_files:
370	        try:
371	            data = json.loads(jf.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
372	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
373	            continue
374	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
375	            continue
376	        key = rule.metadata_key
377	        if key and key in data and data[key] == rule.old_value:
378	            findings.append(
379	                MigrationFinding(
380	                    rule=rule,
381	                    file=jf,
382	                    current_value=data[key],
383	                    proposed_value=rule.new_value,
384	                )
385	            )
386	    return findings
387	
388	
389	def _scan_for_path_format(
390	    json_files: list[Path],
391	    rule: MigrationRule,
392	) -> list[MigrationFinding]:
393	    """Scan for relative paths that should be BIDS URIs."""
394	    findings: list[MigrationFinding] = []
395	    key = rule.metadata_key
396	    if not key:
397	        return findings
398	
399	    for jf in json_files:
400	        try:
401	            data = json.loads(jf.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
402	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
403	            continue
404	        if not isinstance(data, dict) or key not in data:
405	            continue
406	
407	        value = data[key]
408	        paths_to_check: list[str] = []
409	        if isinstance(value, str):
410	            paths_to_check = [value]
411	        elif isinstance(value, list):
412	            paths_to_check = [v for v in value if isinstance(v, str)]
413	
414	        for p in paths_to_check:
415	            if p and not p.startswith("bids:") and "/" in p:
416	                findings.append(
417	                    MigrationFinding(
418	                        rule=rule,
419	                        file=jf,
420	                        current_value=p,
421	                        proposed_value=f"bids::{p}",
422	                    )
423	                )
424	    return findings
425

427	def _scan_for_scandate(
428	    dataset_root: Path,
429	    json_files: list[Path],
430	    rule: MigrationRule,
431	) -> list[MigrationFinding]:
432	    """Scan for ScanDate in JSON sidecars (should move to _scans.tsv)."""
433	    findings: list[MigrationFinding] = []
434	    for jf in json_files:
435	        try:
436	            data = json.loads(jf.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
437	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
438	            continue
439	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
440	            continue
441	        if "ScanDate" in data:
442	            findings.append(
443	                MigrationFinding(
444	                    rule=rule,
445	                    file=jf,
446	                    current_value=f"ScanDate: {data['ScanDate']}",
447	                    proposed_value="Move to acq_time in _scans.tsv",
448	                )
449	            )
450	    return findings

452	
453	def _scan_for_doi_format(
454	    json_files: list[Path],
455	    rule: MigrationRule,
456	) -> list[MigrationFinding]:
457	    """Scan for bare DOIs that should be URI format."""
458	    findings: list[MigrationFinding] = []
459	    for jf in json_files:
460	        if not jf.name.endswith("dataset_description.json"):
461	            continue
462	        try:
463	            data = json.loads(jf.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
464	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
465	            continue
466	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
467	            continue
468	        doi = data.get("DatasetDOI", "")
469	        if isinstance(doi, str) and re.match(r"^10\.", doi):
470	            findings.append(
471	                MigrationFinding(
472	                    rule=rule,
473	                    file=jf,
474	                    current_value=doi,
475	                    proposed_value=f"doi:{doi}",
476	                )
477	            )
478	    return findings
479	
480	
481	def _scan_bids_files(dataset_root: Path) -> list[Path]:
482	    """Find all BIDS data files (non-JSON, non-TSV) in the dataset."""
483	    results: list[Path] = []
484	    for p in sorted(dataset_root.rglob("*")):
485	        if not p.is_file():
486	            continue
487	        # Skip non-BIDS directories
488	        rel = p.relative_to(dataset_root)
489	        parts = rel.parts
490	        if parts and parts[0] in (
491	            "derivatives",
492	            "sourcedata",
493	            "code",
494	            ".git",
495	            ".datalad",
496	        ):
497	            continue
498	        # Skip JSON sidecars, TSV files, and dataset_description
499	        if p.suffix in (".json", ".tsv"):
500	            continue
501	        results.append(p)
502	    return results
503	
504	
505	def _scan_for_suffix_deprecation(
506	    dataset_root: Path,
507	    rule: MigrationRule,
508	) -> list[MigrationFinding]:
509	    """Scan for files with deprecated suffixes."""
510	    findings: list[MigrationFinding] = []
511	    deprecated_suffix = rule.old_value
512	    if not deprecated_suffix:
513	        return findings
514	
515	    bids_files = _scan_bids_files(dataset_root)
516	    for fp in bids_files:
517	        try:
518	            bp = BIDSPath.from_path(fp)
519	        except Exception:
520	            continue
521	        if bp.suffix != deprecated_suffix:
522	            continue
523	
524	        if deprecated_suffix == "phase":
525	            # Auto-fixable: _phase -> _part-phase_bold
526	            findings.append(
527	                MigrationFinding(
528	                    rule=rule,
529	                    file=fp,
530	                    current_value=f"suffix={deprecated_suffix}",
531	                    proposed_value="suffix=bold, part=phase",
532	                    can_auto_fix=True,
533	                )
534	            )
535	        else:
536	            # T2star, FLASH, PD — ambiguous, cannot auto-fix
537	            findings.append(
538	                MigrationFinding(
539	                    rule=rule,
540	                    file=fp,
541	                    current_value=f"suffix={deprecated_suffix}",
542	                    proposed_value=rule.description,
543	                    can_auto_fix=False,
544	                    reason=rule.description,
545	                )
546	            )
547	    return findings
548	
549	
550	def _scan_for_deprecated_template(
551	    json_files: list[Path],
552	    rule: MigrationRule,
553	) -> list[MigrationFinding]:
554	    """Scan for deprecated template identifiers in coordinate system fields."""
555	    findings: list[MigrationFinding] = []
556	    deprecated_value = rule.old_value
557	    if not deprecated_value:
558	        return findings
559	
560	    for jf in json_files:
561	        try:
562	            data = json.loads(jf.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
563	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
564	            continue
565	        if not isinstance(data, dict):
566	            continue
567	
568	        for key in _COORDINATE_SYSTEM_KEYS:
569	            if key in data and data[key] == deprecated_value:
570	                findings.append(
571	                    MigrationFinding(
572	                        rule=rule,
573	                        file=jf,
574	                        current_value=f"{key}={deprecated_value}",
575	                        proposed_value=(
576	                            f"Replace '{deprecated_value}'"
577	                            " with a current template identifier"
578	                        ),
579	                        can_auto_fix=False,
580	                        reason=(
581	                            f"Template '{deprecated_value}' is deprecated;"
582	                            " replacement requires manual selection"
583	                        ),
584	                    )
585	                )
586	    return findings
587

590	# 2.0-specific scanners
591	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
592	
593	
594	def _scan_for_entity_rename(
595	    dataset_root: Path,
596	    rule: MigrationRule,
597	) -> list[MigrationFinding]:
598	    """Scan for files using a deprecated entity key (2.0 migration)."""
599	    findings: list[MigrationFinding] = []
600	    old_key = rule.old_field
601	    new_key = rule.new_field
602	    if not old_key:
603	        return findings
604	
605	    bids_files = _scan_bids_files(dataset_root)
606	    for fp in bids_files:
607	        try:
608	            bp = BIDSPath.from_path(fp)
609	        except Exception:
610	            continue
611	        if old_key in bp.entities:
612	            findings.append(
613	                MigrationFinding(
614	                    rule=rule,
615	                    file=fp,
616	                    current_value=f"{old_key}-{bp.entities[old_key]}",
617	                    proposed_value=f"{new_key}-{bp.entities[old_key]}",
618	                    can_auto_fix=True,
619	                )
620	            )
621	    return findings
622	
623	
624	def _scan_for_metadata_key_change(
625	    json_files: list[Path],
626	    rule: MigrationRule,
627	) -> list[MigrationFinding]:
628	    """Scan for metadata keys that changed in 2.0."""
629	    # Reuse the field_rename scanner — same logic, different category label
630	    return _scan_for_field_rename(json_files, rule)
631	
632	
633	def _scan_for_structural_reorg(
634	    dataset_root: Path,
635	    rule: MigrationRule,
636	) -> list[MigrationFinding]:
637	    """Scan for structural layout issues requiring 2.0 reorganization.
638	
639	    Structural reorganization rules are inherently ambiguous and require
640	    human judgment.  This scanner flags findings but marks them as not
641	    auto-fixable.
642	    """
643	    findings: list[MigrationFinding] = []
644	    # Structural reorg rules describe directory layout changes that cannot
645	    # be applied automatically without understanding dataset intent.
646	    # Flag the entire dataset as needing review.
647	    findings.append(
648	        MigrationFinding(
649	            rule=rule,
650	            file=dataset_root / "dataset_description.json",
651	            current_value="current layout",
652	            proposed_value=rule.description,
653	            can_auto_fix=False,
654	            reason=(
655	                "Structural reorganization requires human judgment;"
656	                " review the BIDS 2.0 specification for guidance"
657	            ),
658	        )
659	    )
660	    return findings
661	
662	
663	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
664	# 2.0-specific appliers
665	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
666	
667	
668	def _apply_entity_rename(
669	    finding: MigrationFinding, dataset: BIDSDataset
670	) -> Change | None:
671	    """Apply an entity key rename by delegating to rename_file()."""
672	    from bids_utils.rename import rename_file
673	
674	    fp = finding.file
675	    rule = finding.rule
676	    old_key = rule.old_field
677	    new_key = rule.new_field
678	    if not old_key or not new_key:
679	        return None
680	
681	    try:
682	        bp = BIDSPath.from_path(fp)
683	    except Exception:
684	        return None
685	
686	    if old_key not in bp.entities:
687	        return None
688	
689	    # Rename: drop old entity, add new entity with same value
690	    value = bp.entities[old_key]
691	    result = rename_file(
692	        dataset,
693	        fp,
694	        set_entities={new_key: value},
695	        drop_entities=[old_key],
696	    )
697	    if result.success and result.changes:
698	        return result.changes[0]
699	    return None
700	
701	
702	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
703	# Apply fixes
704	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
705	
706	
707	def _apply_field_rename(finding: MigrationFinding) -> Change | None:
708	    """Apply a metadata field rename."""
709	    jf = finding.file
710	    try:
711	        data = json.loads(jf.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
712	    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
713	        return None
714	    rule = finding.rule
715	    if rule.old_field and rule.old_field in data:
716	        value = data.pop(rule.old_field)
717	        # Merge into new field (handle Sources consolidation)
718	        if rule.new_field:
719	            existing = data.get(rule.new_field)
720	            if existing is not None:
721	                # Merge lists
722	                if isinstance(existing, list) and isinstance(value, list):
723	                    data[rule.new_field] = existing + value
724	                elif isinstance(existing, list):
725	                    data[rule.new_field] = existing + [value]
726	                # else: existing value takes precedence
727	            else:
728	                data[rule.new_field] = value
729	        jf.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
730	        return Change(
731	            action="modify",
732	            source=jf,
733	            detail=f"Renamed field {rule.old_field} → {rule.new_field}",
734	        )
735	    return None
736	
737	
738	def _apply_enum_rename(finding: MigrationFinding) -> Change | None:
739	    """Apply an enum value rename."""
740	    jf = finding.file
741	    try:
742	        data = json.loads(jf.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
743	    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
744	        return None
745	    rule = finding.rule
746	    key = rule.metadata_key
747	    if key and key in data and data[key] == rule.old_value:
748	        data[key] = rule.new_value
749	        jf.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
750	        return Change(
751	            action="modify",
752	            source=jf,
753	            detail=f"Updated {key}: {rule.old_value} → {rule.new_value}",
754	        )
755	    return None
756	
757	
758	def _apply_path_format(finding: MigrationFinding) -> Change | None:
759	    """Convert relative path to BIDS URI."""
760	    jf = finding.file
761	    try:
762	        data = json.loads(jf.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
763	    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
764	        return None
765	    rule = finding.rule
766	    key = rule.metadata_key
767	    if not key or key not in data:
768	        return None
769	
770	    modified = False
771	    value = data[key]
772	    if isinstance(value, str) and not value.startswith("bids:") and "/" in value:
773	        data[key] = f"bids::{value}"
774	        modified = True
775	    elif isinstance(value, list):
776	        new_list = []
777	        for v in value:
778	            if isinstance(v, str) and not v.startswith("bids:") and "/" in v:
779	                new_list.append(f"bids::{v}")
780	                modified = True
781	            else:
782	                new_list.append(v)
783	        data[key] = new_list
784	
785	    if modified:
786	        jf.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
787	        return Change(
788	            action="modify",
789	            source=jf,
790	            detail=f"Converted {key} to BIDS URI format",
791	        )
792	    return None
793	
794	
795	def _apply_scandate_move(
796	    finding: MigrationFinding, dataset_root: Path
797	) -> Change | None:
798	    """Move ScanDate from JSON to _scans.tsv acq_time."""
799	    jf = finding.file
800	    try:
801	        data = json.loads(jf.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
802	    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
803	        return None
804	
805	    scan_date = data.pop("ScanDate", None)
806	    if scan_date is None:
807	        return None
808	
809	    jf.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
810	
811	    # Try to find the corresponding _scans.tsv and update acq_time
812	    scans_path = find_scans_tsv(jf, dataset_root)
813	    if scans_path is not None:
814	        rows = read_scans_tsv(scans_path)
815	        # Find the data file that corresponds to this JSON
816	        stem = jf.stem  # e.g., sub-01_bold
817	        for row in rows:
818	            fn = row.get("filename", "")
819	            if fn.replace(".nii.gz", "").replace(".nii", "").endswith(stem):
820	                if not row.get("acq_time"):
821	                    row["acq_time"] = scan_date
822	                break
823	        write_scans_tsv(scans_path, rows)
824	
825	    return Change(
826	        action="modify",
827	        source=jf,
828	        detail=f"Moved ScanDate ({scan_date}) to _scans.tsv acq_time",
829	    )
830	
831	
832	def _apply_doi_format(finding: MigrationFinding) -> Change | None:
833	    """Convert bare DOI to URI format."""
834	    jf = finding.file
835	    try:
836	        data = json.loads(jf.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
837	    except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
838	        return None
839	    doi = data.get("DatasetDOI", "")
840	    if isinstance(doi, str) and re.match(r"^10\.", doi):
841	        data["DatasetDOI"] = f"doi:{doi}"
842	        jf.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
843	        return Change(
844	            action="modify",
845	            source=jf,
846	            detail=f"Converted DatasetDOI to URI format: doi:{doi}",
847	        )
848	    return None
849	
850	
851	def _apply_suffix_deprecation(
852	    finding: MigrationFinding, dataset: BIDSDataset
853	) -> Change | None:
854	    """Apply suffix deprecation fix by delegating to rename_file()."""
855	    from bids_utils.rename import rename_file
856	
857	    fp = finding.file
858	    bp = BIDSPath.from_path(fp)
859	
860	    if bp.suffix == "phase":
861	        # _phase -> _part-phase_bold
862	        result = rename_file(
863	            dataset,
864	            fp,
865	            set_entities={"part": "phase"},
866	            new_suffix="bold",
867	        )
868	        if result.success and result.changes:
869	            return result.changes[0]
870	    return None
871	
872	
873	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
874	# Main orchestrator
875	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
876	
877	
878	def migrate_dataset(
879	    dataset: BIDSDataset,
880	    *,
881	    to_version: str | None = None,
882	    dry_run: bool = False,
883	) -> MigrationResult:
884	    """Apply schema-driven migrations to a BIDS dataset.
885	
886	    When the target is a major version upgrade (e.g., 1.x → 2.0), migration
887	    is **cumulative**: all 1.x deprecation fixes are applied first, then
888	    2.0-specific transformations.
889	
890	    Parameters
891	    ----------
892	    dataset
893	        The BIDS dataset to migrate.
894	    to_version
895	        Target BIDS version. If None, defaults to the current schema version.
896	    dry_run
897	        If True, scan and report findings without modifying files.
898	
899	    Returns
900	    -------
901	    MigrationResult
902	        Findings and changes made (or planned).
903	    """
904	    from_version = dataset.bids_version
905	
906	    if to_version is None:
907	        # Default to the schema's version
908	        to_version = dataset.schema.bids_version
909	
910	    result = MigrationResult(
911	        dry_run=dry_run,
912	        from_version=from_version,
913	        to_version=to_version,
914	    )
915	
916	    is_major_upgrade = _is_major_version_upgrade(from_version, to_version)
917	
918	    if is_major_upgrade:
919	        # Cumulative migration: apply all 1.x fixes first, then 2.0 rules
920	        latest_1x = _latest_1x_version()
921	        onex_rules = _get_rules(from_version, latest_1x)
922	        twox_rules = _get_rules(from_version, to_version, major_only=True)
923	        rules = onex_rules + twox_rules
924	    else:
925	        rules = _get_rules(from_version, to_version)
926	
927	    if not rules:
928	        result.warnings.append("No applicable migration rules found")
929	        return result
930	
931	    # Scan all JSON files
932	    json_files = _scan_json_files(dataset.root)
933	
934	    # Scan for findings per rule category
935	    scanners: dict[str, Callable[..., list[MigrationFinding]]] = {
936	        "field_rename": lambda r: _scan_for_field_rename(json_files, r),
937	        "enum_rename": lambda r: _scan_for_enum_rename(json_files, r),
938	        "path_format": lambda r: _scan_for_path_format(json_files, r),
939	        "cross_file_move": lambda r: _scan_for_scandate(dataset.root, json_files, r),
940	        "value_rename": lambda r: _scan_for_doi_format(json_files, r),
941	        "suffix_deprecation": lambda r: _scan_for_suffix_deprecation(dataset.root, r),
942	        "deprecated_template": lambda r: _scan_for_deprecated_template(json_files, r),
943	        # 2.0-specific categories
944	        "entity_rename": lambda r: _scan_for_entity_rename(dataset.root, r),
945	        "metadata_key_change": lambda r: _scan_for_metadata_key_change(json_files, r),
946	        "structural_reorg": lambda r: _scan_for_structural_reorg(dataset.root, r),
947	    }
948	
949	    for rule in rules:
950	        scanner = scanners.get(rule.category)
951	        if scanner:
952	            findings = scanner(rule)
953	            result.findings.extend(findings)
954	
955	    if not result.findings:
956	        result.warnings.append("Nothing to migrate — dataset is up to date")
957	        return result
958	
959	    # T043: Check for ambiguities that should abort migration
960	    unfixable = [f for f in result.findings if not f.can_auto_fix]
961	    if is_major_upgrade and unfixable and not dry_run:
962	        # For major version upgrades, unfixable findings abort the migration
963	        # rather than partially applying (user must resolve ambiguities first)
964	        result.success = False
965	        for f in unfixable:
966	            result.errors.append(
967	                f"Cannot auto-fix ({f.rule.id}): {f.file}: {f.reason}"
968	            )
969	        result.warnings.append(
970	            "Migration aborted: resolve the above ambiguities manually "
971	            "before migrating to a new major version. "
972	            "Run with --dry-run to see all findings."
973	        )
974	        return result
975	
976	    if dry_run:
977	        return result
978	
979	    # Apply fixes
980	    appliers: dict[str, Callable[..., Change | None]] = {
981	        "field_rename": lambda f: _apply_field_rename(f),
982	        "enum_rename": lambda f: _apply_enum_rename(f),
983	        "path_format": lambda f: _apply_path_format(f),
984	        "cross_file_move": lambda f: _apply_scandate_move(f, dataset.root),
985	        "value_rename": lambda f: _apply_doi_format(f),
986	        "suffix_deprecation": lambda f: _apply_suffix_deprecation(f, dataset),
987	        # 2.0-specific appliers
988	        "entity_rename": lambda f: _apply_entity_rename(f, dataset),
989	        "metadata_key_change": lambda f: _apply_field_rename(f),
990	        # deprecated_template, structural_reorg: no applier — can_auto_fix=False
991	    }
992	
993	    for finding in result.findings:
994	        if not finding.can_auto_fix:
995	            result.warnings.append(f"Cannot auto-fix: {finding.file}: {finding.reason}")
996	            continue
997	
998	        applier = appliers.get(finding.rule.category)
999	        if applier:
1000	            change = applier(finding)
1001	            if change:
1002	                result.changes.append(change)
1003	
1004	    return result
1005
